channel.title: Bloomberg Green Global
channel.description: Últimos artigos de Bloomberg Green Global

item.title: Rhinos Return to Billionaire-Backed Park After 1990s Wipe Out
item.description: Black rhinos are being reintroduced into a billionaire-backed national park in Zimbabwe’s Zambezi Valley three decades after almost all of them were evacuated to protect them from poachers.
item.dc:creator: Antony Sguazzin

item.title: Uber Targets Doubling Its Fleet of Electric Motorcycles in Kenya
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc plans to double its fleet of electric motorcycles in Kenya by the end of the year, as the company races toward becoming a zero-emissions mobility platform globally by 2040.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ombok

item.title: BNP Manager Who Slammed ‘Meme-Like’ SMR Rally Is Now a Buyer
item.description: A fund run by BNP Paribas Asset Management that’s outperformed 97% of its peers over the past year says it’s now moving into small-modular-reactor stocks after long shunning the sector as a “non-viable” investment.
item.dc:creator: Ishika Mookerjee

item.title: Natural-Disaster Insurance Gap Now Exceeds $420 Billion Globally
item.description: The value of uninsured natural-catastrophe losses rose more than 7% globally last year to $424 billion, with North America the most exposed region, according to a fresh analysis.
item.dc:creator: Laura Millan

item.title: Flights Halted as Tropical Storm Jangmi Nears Tokyo
item.description: Tropical storm Jangmi approached Tokyo on Wednesday, bringing strong winds and heavy rain that triggered hundreds of flight cancellations as well as flood warnings around the capital.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Reynolds, Natsuko Katsuki

item.title: Extreme Weather to Drive $20 Trillion in Spending, BI Says
item.description: Extreme weather will likely spur more than $20 trillion in global spending over the next decade, boosting sales and earnings for reinsurers and companies involved in energy efficiency and climate security, according to analysts at Bloomberg Intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Raimonde

item.title: US Nuclear Fuel Enricher Scales Up to Offset Russia Uranium Ban
item.description: Urenco USA, the only commercial-scale nuclear fuel producer in the US, aims to lift its capacity to make enriched uranium by almost 50% through a multibillion expansion project as America moves to wean itself off of Russian uranium.
item.dc:creator: John Ainger, Will Wade

item.title: Data Centers Now Use So Much Power, Their Designs Are Changing
item.description: The huge amount of power that data centers use is forcing a rethink of their form.
item.dc:creator: Emily Forgash, Rachael Dottle

item.title: EDF Mulls Adding Cooling Towers to Nuclear Plants Amid Warming
item.description: Electricite de France SA is considering adding extra cooling towers at some of its nuclear reactors most exposed to rising river temperatures as the state-owned utility adapts its fleet to a warming climate.
item.dc:creator: Francois de Beaupuy

item.title: PE-Owned JetBio Plans Landmark Green Jet Fuel Plant in Brazil
item.description: Private equity-controlled JetBio plans to build the world’s biggest commercial green jet fuel plant that uses ethanol in Brazil to take advantage of the country’s ample feedstock alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Ana Mano
